caption:

2. Click "Mood Boards" on the bottom dashboard
3. Drop in a batch of images that share the aesthetic you want
4. Let Krea analyze the palette and mood
5. Prompt whatever you want and it will match your Mood Board perfectly

Krea V2 just dropped and it's perfect for if you’re building a brand, designing a project, or want AI to mimic your own artwork.

➡️ Share this with a creative friend and SAVE it to so you don't forget to test it out later!
transcript: If you're looking for a brand new image model, well, we got a new one out of CreAI this week in CreAI 2. This model to me seems like it has a lot of the sort of functionality and controllability that we get out of Midjourney. Like you can give it an image and tell it to style it like that image, but then you can also control how much it gets stylized like that image using like a little slider thing. And then if you give it multiple images, you can control, you know, each of the weightings of the various styles that you give it here. They also explain that if you give it a very basic prompt, like a cat riding a bicycle, it'll give you a whole bunch of different styles. Like every image is going to be wildly different from every other image. So, you can kind of go down your own creative workflow. And then once you find a style that you really like, you drag it in, continue to prompt more details, but it follows the initial style that you found. They've also got what they call mood boards, where you can give it a bunch of images that sort of all have a similar style. You can see it'll analyze the mood board, and then it'll give it a taste profile, some keywords, and what to avoid. And then you can generate images with that mood board, and any images that you generate with that mood board will feel like they fit right in with the mood of that. So, if you're looking to like really drill in on a specific style, or maybe you're an artist yourself, you can load in a bunch of your own images and then generate more that match the style that you've already created. So, pretty cool model if you want to dial in like this exact look and style that you're going for.

full_text_english:
🚨 OpenAI just dropped the feature we've all been waiting for: Codex on mobile.

If you watched my recent video on how I built my custom wiki, you know how much I’ve been relying on Codex. So I was super excited for this feature and I know a bunch of other people have been asking for it as well.

comments: Bro… the real time translating frustrated language into professional corporate speak is both hilarious and dystopian.😂

Interaction models are so interesting. I've always thought "proactive audio" (as Gemini calls it), where the model waits to speak, is such a useful feature that's not available as often as it should be. Focusing on a model that's designed not only for that, but for more complex understanding of conversational flow, turn-taking, interruption granting, timing, etc. is a wonderful area of exploration.

And in the article they mention it's actually two models: the realtime interaction model that stays present with the user, and a slower-but-more-capable background model for longer thinking, that both feed into each other. Basically, one model makes quick decisions and acts while the other model thinks more about it and informs updates and error corrections on the faster one. What's especially interesting about that is this is also how humans work. There's a concept in psychology of "system 1 vs system 2" decision-making. System 1 is faster, but less accurate, and system 2 is slower but more accurate; humans will typically act on system 1 decisions immediately, instinctively, and then course-correct with system 2 decisions as they consider the task at hand more. So having a faster model perform the actions/speaking/etc. while a slower model thinks more deeply (and uses tools to research) and then course-corrects the faster one... it's a very human way to handle things. I'm excited to see how this scales.

The Monet experiment is both amusing and also incredibly depressing and frustrating. People are convinced AI is awful and will rationalize that a million ways to Sunday; but they have no idea what's actually AI and what's not, nor how AI even works. They're rationalizing after the fact based on an emotional response of fear and anger instilled in them by others saying "AI steals real things but can't make anything of its own" -- which is of course untrue to begin with. And this happens all the time. And I want to punch those people, but I'm not allowed 😅

caption:
I'm always being asked to pick my favorite AI model, but the truth is my favorite changes all the time based on what's out there.

At the moment, GPT-5.5 is probably my go-to for almost everything like coding, brainstorming, and general questions.

But a month ago I probably would’ve said Claude Opus, and before that I really liked Gemini.
Same thing with image models. Right now I’m loving GPT-Images 2.0, especially for prompts with text. But Nano Banana 2 was the frontrunner for a while.

#AI #aimodels
transcript: If you had to choose only one LLM, ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Claude, Copilot, Grok, or ZAI, which one are you rolling with and why? Same for image and video models, you can't say Leonardo, no cheating. Okay, so when it comes to this question, I have zero zero loyalty. Like, I will use ChatGPT when I feel like it is the best model of the moment. I will go and use the Claude models if I feel like they're the best models of the moment. As of this recording, GPT 5.5 is my go-to, but if you would have asked me last month, it was probably Claude Opus 4.7. I just use whatever I feel is the best model in the moment. At one point, I really liked the conversation abilities of ChatGPT, but the coding abilities of Claude, so I would jump between them based on what I was using it for. Right now, I kind of find GPT 5.5 to be good at almost everything, so I'm primarily using that. Will my opinion change next week or next month or 3 months from now? Absolutely. It will probably keep changing as new models come out. Same for image and video models. If you would have asked me a month ago, it was Nano Banana Pro. That model was the best of the best for me. You ask me today, I really really like the GPT image model, especially if I want text in it. Again, zero loyalty.

hook_text_english: Mira Murati's texts to Sam Altman during his firing was the Musk trial's biggest bombshell
full_text_english:
Former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati's texts to Sam Altman when he was briefly fired from OpenAI was by far the biggest bombshell to come out of the Musk vs Altman trial so far.

For context: When Sam Altman was suddenly ousted from OpenAI in 2023 and rehired a few days later, the public story made it sound almost strategic and calculated.

These texts between Altman and Murati revealed the opposite. 

They showed all the confusion and panic happening behind the scenes and how Satya Nadella, Microsoft's CEO, was pulled in to basically mediate the whole mess.

All in all, not a good look for OpenAI or Sam, but we'll see what else this trial reveals as it continues on. 

And now it seems like "Directionally very bad" may be the new buzz phrase of the season 😂

#AI #ainews #technews #openai #chatgpt

hook_text_english: Claude inside Microsoft Office is an AI feature that actually makes a lot of sense
full_text_english:
➡️ Share this with someone who would actually use it!

Claude inside Microsoft Office is an AI feature that actually makes a lot or sense.

You can now use Claude directly inside Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook, and it'll remember context across all of the apps.

So for example, you can ask Claude to draft an email in Outlook, jump into Word to turn that email into a proposal, then move into Excel to analyze the numbers behind it. That's an actually useful, real-life scenario where AI can save you a lot of time and make your life easier. 

Honestly one of the more practical AI integrations I’ve seen lately.

#AI #Claude #Anthropic #AInews #ProductivityHacks

Anthropic just partnered with SpaceX which could directly impact Claude users by raising usage limits for Claude Code and the API.

But the reason this is such a twist is because Elon Musk, the same guy that has been publicly been taking shots at Anthropic, is now helping power it behind the scenes.

Meanwhile Anthropic also committed something like $200 BILLION to Google Cloud compute.

They're actually raising the usage limits for Claude. That's like the main reason we're hearing most people going back to ChatGPT. But, the other one is one that I don't think anybody really saw coming. [music] And that's that Claude signed a compute deal with SpaceX. And then, the other deal they're talking about is that they committed to spending $200 billion on Google's cloud. So, they're getting compute from Google, and they're getting compute from SpaceX. One of the reasons this feels so interesting is cuz, well, Elon Musk hasn't really been a fan of Anthropic, calling Anthropic misanthropic only, you know, 2 months ago here. But, fast-forward to today, and Elon and Dario seem to be making deals together. Elon's also said something like the amount of compute they have, they're only using like 11% of it. So, might as well make some money off of all of this excess compute that is not getting used by XAI, which is now, I guess, getting rebranded as SpaceXAI. True story. Look it up.
